strategy: The fight is easy once you respect the cheats: Takanobu Disarms any blade you cast, so buff with traps instead of blades.
Stack Feints on him, then have your hitter cash them in; the guide says this can be done in 1-2 turns by spamming Feints for the hitter.
Prefer a single-hit, single-target attack for the kill. Any all-enemies spell triggers a guaranteed 560 Storm Tempest on your team, and multi-hit spells trigger one dispel per hit.
Do not cast Storm shields (he answers with a Storm Prism on you) and mind his pips: at 6+ pips he opens with Darkwind, and at 5+ pips he ends the round with Supercharge, so kill fast.

Watch the Bladeless Ronin dispels: any Balance damage triggers Unbalance, any Fire/Ice/Storm damage triggers the matching Quench/Melt/Dissipate, and any Myth/Life/Death damage triggers Vaporize/Entangle/Strangle on your whole team.
A Damage-over-Time hit sidesteps all of it: DoT damage does not trigger the Ronins' dispel cheats, making a DoT hitter the cleanest kill. Hit with Myth (+40%) around his 70% Storm resist.
cheatNotes: Cast any blade and Takanobu instantly interrupt-casts Disarm on whoever received it, so buff with traps and Feints rather than blades.
A multi-target blade only loses the caster's copy; teammates keep theirs, so mass blades are the safer way to blade if you must.
Casting any Storm shield makes him interrupt-cast a Storm Prism on the shielded wizard, turning your Storm resist into a weakness.
Any all-enemies spell triggers a guaranteed Tempest that always deals 560 Storm damage to your team.
If he has 6 or more pips at the start of a round he interrupt-casts Darkwind (accuracy debuff), so keep his pips down or expect fizzles.
If he ends a round with 5 or more pips he Supercharges himself for extra pips, so pressure him fast.
Deal any Balance damage and the Dagger Ronin hits your whole team with Unbalance (a Balance dispel).
Deal Fire, Ice, or Storm damage and the Key Ronin casts the matching all-enemies dispel: Quench, Melt, or Dissipate.
Deal Myth, Life, or Death damage and the Gem Ronin casts the matching all-enemies dispel: Vaporize, Entangle, or Strangle.
Damage-over-Time effects do not trigger the Ronins' dispel cheats, so a DoT hitter avoids all of them.
A spell that deals damage more than once triggers one dispel per hit, applied in the order the hits landed, so avoid multi-hit spells.
A spell hitting multiple enemies makes the relevant Ronin repeat its dispel cheat once per enemy struck.
Casting Hydra pulls all three elemental dispels from the Key Ronin: Quench, then Melt, then Dissipate.
Casting Scales of Destiny triggers Entangle from the Gem Ronin, Melt from the Key Ronin, and Unbalance from the Dagger Ronin, in that order.
Hitting through a Prism makes the Ronin cast the dispel matching the school after the prism converts, not your base school.
